name: Saint Lucia

colors.symbolism: the surrounding sea and the clear tropical sky
the golden sunshine of the Caribbean and the prosperity of the islanders
the white-skinned heritage and the cultural influence of one of the founding peoples; together with black, the harmony between the country's two principal racial groups
the black-skinned heritage and African ancestry of the majority of the population; together with white, the harmony between the country's two principal racial groups

description: A cerulean-blue field bearing a centred device of three superimposed triangles: a yellow isosceles triangle resting on its base, behind a tall black isosceles triangle bordered by a thin white fimbriation. The composite shape evokes the twin Pitons rising from a golden beach and surrounded by sea and sky.
history: Designed by the Saint Lucian artist Dunstan St Omer, the flag was first adopted on 1 March 1967 when Saint Lucia became an associated state of the United Kingdom and was retained — with minor adjustments to the shade of blue and the proportions of the triangles — at independence on 22 February 1979. Further refinements were made in 2002.

features.symbolism: the cerulean blue field represents the surrounding Caribbean Sea and Atlantic Ocean and the clear tropical sky

three superimposed triangles — a tall black isoceles triangle bordered with white, set on a yellow triangle — representing the twin Pitons (Gros Piton and Petit Piton), Saint Lucia's iconic volcanic peaks rising from the sea; the black-and-white juxtaposition also signifies the harmony between the country's principal racial groups, and the yellow triangle below evokes sunshine and prosperity
